evals.prompt: fire up the android emulator and run my flutter app, then tell me what tabs are on the home screen
evals.expected_output: Agent boots the AVD, launches the Flutter app via the skill's run command, blocks on attach, then reports the bottom-nav labels by reading the accessibility tree (not by screenshotting).
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh boot before scripts/emu.sh run.
Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh run followed by scripts/emu.sh wait-run (no `sleep` substituted for wait-run).
Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh ui-list to read on-screen labels.
Transcript does NOT invoke scripts/emu.sh screenshot before ui-list (screenshot is ~30x more tokens than ui-list; ui-list is the documented default).
Final answer enumerates tab labels, not pixel coordinates.

evals.prompt: the bottom nav lists Home, Search, Library, Profile — tap Search and tell me what page comes up.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses tap-label (the documented preferred method), then confirms the result by re-reading the accessibility tree.
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh tap-label with a string containing 'Search'.
Transcript does NOT invoke scripts/emu.sh tap with raw X Y coordinates for this action.
If the actual node label is multi-line (e.g. 'Search\nTab 2 of 4'), the agent passes only 'Search' — substring match is enough per the skill docs.
Transcript verifies the result with scripts/emu.sh ui-list (not screenshot).

evals.prompt: long-press the Profile tab on the bottom nav so the context menu appears.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses the skill's hold or hold-label command — NOT a same-coord swipe — because adb's long-press-via-swipe is read by Flutter as a cancelled tap and never fires onLongPress.
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh hold-label (or scripts/emu.sh hold X Y MS).
Transcript does NOT invoke `adb shell input swipe` with identical or near-identical start/end coords as a way to fake a long-press.
Transcript does NOT invoke scripts/emu.sh swipe with identical or near-identical start/end coords for the same purpose.

evals.prompt: pinch out to zoom in on the map view in the running app — roughly the center of the screen, about 2x.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses the skill's pinch command (which routes through the qemu console) rather than trying to script multi-touch via adb.
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh pinch out (with or without center/start-gap/end-gap arguments).
Transcript does NOT attempt multi-touch via `adb shell input` (it is single-touch only and will not produce a real pinch).
Transcript does NOT attempt `adb shell sendevent` or other root-required workarounds.

evals.prompt: I'm done debugging — stop the flutter run session and shut down the emulator.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses scripts/emu.sh kill-run for the backgrounded daemon (not TaskStop), then scripts/emu.sh exit for the emulator.
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh kill-run.

Transcript does NOT use TaskStop on the background flutter run task as the primary stop mechanism — the SKILL.md notes the task may already be reaped, and either way the app keeps running on the device until force-stopped.
Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh exit after kill-run to shut the AVD down.

evals.prompt: I just ran `scripts/emu.sh ui-list` against my running flutter app and it printed `(no labelled nodes found — is Flutter semantics enabled?)`. The app is rendering fine on screen — what's wrong, and how do I fix it so I can use tap-label?
evals.expected_output: Agent diagnoses the missing Flutter semantics tree and recommends fixing the app — adding `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` to main() in debug builds, and/or wrapping icon-only widgets in Semantics(label: ...). Does NOT recommend coordinate-guessing or screenshotting as the primary path forward.
assertions: Answer recommends calling `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` in main() (typically guarded by kDebugMode).
Answer mentions wrapping custom or icon-only widgets in `Semantics(label: ...)` (or `identifier:`) for cases not auto-covered by Material widgets.
Answer does NOT propose 'just tap by coordinates from a screenshot' as the primary fix.
Answer optionally mentions enabling TalkBack on the AVD as the heavier alternative.

evals.prompt: take a screenshot of the emulator — there's a popup blocking the home screen and I want to see what it says.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs scripts/emu.sh screenshot, reads the JPEG path the command printed on stdout, and Reads that exact path. Does NOT hardcode `/tmp/android-emu-shot.jpg` — the script writes per-invocation paths suffixed with the script PID for safe concurrent use.
assertions: Transcript invokes scripts/emu.sh screenshot.
Transcript reads the file path that screenshot printed on stdout (matches `/tmp/android-emu-shot-.+\.jpg`).
Transcript does NOT pass a hardcoded `/tmp/android-emu-shot.jpg` literal to the Read tool.
Agent uses the Read tool on the printed JPEG path to actually look at the popup before answering.
